content:
- Completed task 40 (ultra-high): migrated repo gates to only `make test` (regular) + `make test-long` (ultra-long-only).
- Makefile: introduced `ULTRA_LONG_TESTS` / `ULTRA_LONG_SKIP_ARGS`; `make test` now skips only `ha::e2e_multi_node::e2e_multi_node_stress_no_quorum_fencing_with_concurrent_sql`; removed redundant `--include-ignored` pass; `make test-long` now runs only ULTRA_LONG_TESTS with warning message.
- Repo text sweep: updated `.ralph/ralph-do-task.md`, `RUST_SYSTEM_HARNESS_PLAN.md`, all `.agents/skills/*` task templates, and a large number of `.ralph/tasks/**` to remove duplicated `make test` gate lists, fix drifted `make-test.log` duplicates, and replace legacy “BDD is a separate suite” wording with “BDD features pass (covered by `make test`).”.
- Ran gates (all green):
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 make check`
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 make test` (twice)
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 make lint` (twice, once after test-long)
  - `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 make test-long` (no-quorum fencing finished ~200s)
- Noted pitfall: used `rg` with patterns containing markdown backticks inside double quotes; that triggered shell command substitution and accidentally ran `make test`. Added learnings to `AGENTS.md` (use single quotes; also rg here lacks PCRE2 so avoid `rg -P`).
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed with required message, and pushed.

- Read previous progress log and inspected `.ralph/current_tasks.md`.
- Verified there are no bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>` / `<passing>false</passing>` in the current summary.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks still not started with `<passes>false</passes>` and one recurring `<passes>meta-task</passes>`.
- Applied priority + story-chain rules: selected the only remaining `ultra-high` failing task in `story-rust-system-harness`.
- Wrote `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md` to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Next: stop here (Choose-Task agent) so the next agent can execute task 41.

- Subagent analysis: inspected real-binary HA harness parallel-safety paths in src/test_harness/{ports,namespace,etcd3,ha_e2e,startup,util} and src/ha/{e2e_multi_node,e2e_partition_chaos}. 
- Found major TOCTOU port-race pattern: reserved ports are released before bind/start (allocate_ha_topology_ports(...).into_layout(), allocate_non_overlapping_ports(...)->Vec<u16>), making default cargo parallelism vulnerable to cross-test bind collisions/interference.
- Verified namespace/temp-dir and socket-dir isolation are generally good; biggest remaining collision amplifier is static scope/cluster names in e2e fixtures (scope-ha-e2e / scope-ha-e2e-partition), which should be uniquified per fixture to reduce blast radius if any misrouting occurs.
- Recommended API changes: hold reservations through startup with a startup port-plan object, explicit phased release just-in-time, and unique per-fixture scope/cluster/test labels; plus a parallel-stress harness test to validate N concurrent cluster startups.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> task 41: `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md`.
- Spawned subagents to research: (1) current ultra-long no-quorum fencing stress test assertions/split points, (2) HA harness parallel-safety, (3) Makefile timeout mechanics.
- Key finding: HA harness port allocation has a TOCTOU hazard (`allocate_ha_topology_ports(...).into_layout()` and `allocate_non_overlapping_ports(...)->Vec<u16>` drop reservations well before bind), which can flake under default `cargo test` parallelism; also fixtures use fixed `scope`/`cluster_name` increasing interference radius on miswire.
- Wrote a detailed end-to-end execution plan into the task file, including: just-in-time port reservation releases, unique scope/cluster naming, new short no-quorum fencing tests replacing the 3min+ stress test, Makefile 120s timeout for `make test`, and a mapping doc.
- Marked the task file with `TO BE VERIFIED` and stopped as instructed.
- Next: follow the workflow: do a DEEP/SKEPTICAL review of the plan (must change at least one thing), then replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Deep skeptical review on port reservation TOCTOU done.
- Verified hazard concretely: `start_cluster_inner` drops topology reservation via `into_layout()` before any process/proxy bind, and `allocate_non_overlapping_ports` returns `Vec<u16>` which drops reservation immediately.
- Smallest safe refactor is NOT a large startup-port-plan type; instead add release-by-port on reservation objects, keep reservations alive in startup, and release each port immediately before each bind/spawn site.
- Suggested plan changes: replace `take(index)` API idea with `release_port(u16)`; convert `allocate_non_overlapping_ports` -> reservation-returning helper; in startup release etcd ports before `spawn_etcd3_cluster`, release node/api/proxy ports immediately before each runtime/proxy spawn; keep unique scope/cluster-name hardening as secondary mitigation.

- Performed deep skeptical review of `e2e_multi_node_stress_no_quorum_fencing_with_concurrent_sql` in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`.
- Extracted exact assertions and timing knobs from code: workload setup, etcd majority stop, wait_for_all_failsafe(90s), 8s HA sampling, 7s extra sleep, post-cutoff commit tolerance, split-brain evidence checks, table key integrity.
- Pulled concrete historical timings from `.ralph/evidence/27-e2e-ha-stress/*.summary.json`: passed duration range 126357..297266 ms (n=9), one timeout failure at 300s.
- Computed phase timing distribution: failsafe-observed offset 63..255s from start (median ~126s), post-failsafe tail 25..63s (median ~45s).
- Found critical semantic mismatch: `wait_for_all_failsafe` returns when `!has_primary && observed_failsafe_nodes` (at least one node), not all nodes.
- Found likely runtime inflation vector: fail-safe polling is serial per-node and uses up-to-20s HTTP timeout path, which can turn transient transport errors into long observation delays.
- Proposed split plan will require (a) strict all-node failsafe helper, (b) concurrent HA polling, (c) tighter per-step timeouts for no-quorum tests, and (d) 3 short focused tests with per-test budget ~35-45s so parallel wall-clock stays <120s.
- Next if continuing task flow: write this skeptical delta into task plan, replacing weak assumptions with concrete runtime budgets and required helper changes.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> task 41 `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/41-task-ultra-high-prio-split-ultra-long-e2e-into-short-parallel-tests.md`.
- Found task file already had a detailed plan ending with `TO BE VERIFIED`.
- Did deep/skeptical review with 3 explorer subagents:
  - Verified real TOCTOU hazards: `start_cluster_inner` consumes `allocate_ha_topology_ports(...).into_layout()` early; `allocate_non_overlapping_ports` drops reservations via `into_vec()`.
  - Extracted exact assertions + timing evidence from the ultra-long no-quorum fencing test; noted `wait_for_all_failsafe` is not strict-all-nodes.
  - Reviewed Makefile; proposed portable `timeout`/`gtimeout` wrapper + guard that `RUST_TEST_THREADS=1` is disallowed.
- Updated the plan in the task file (required alteration) and replaced marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.
  - Simplified port plan to use `PortReservation::release_port(u16)` (smaller surface than index-based take).
  - Removed heavy real-binary “start N clusters concurrently” regression test; replaced with focused unit tests for `release_port` semantics.
  - Reduced no-quorum replacement suite to 2 short tests (still multiple) to limit parallel resource contention; added strict-all-nodes failsafe helper requirement.
  - Expanded Makefile plan to include `ensure-timeout`, kill-after, and explicit serial-guard.
- Next: execute the plan as written (no more exploration) and tick boxes while implementing.

- Started executing task 41 plan (NOW EXECUTE).
- Baseline: inspected ultra-long no-quorum fencing test in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and current Makefile targets.
- Implemented port reservation TOCTOU fix:
  - Added PortReservation::empty() and PortReservation::release_port(u16) plus HaTopologyPortReservation::release_port.
  - Replaced allocate_non_overlapping_ports -> reserve_non_overlapping_ports returning a PortReservation (keeps listeners alive).
  - Refactored src/test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs to keep topology reservation alive, release etcd/api/pg/proxy ports just-in-time before bind/spawn.
  - Updated spawn_partition_etcd_proxies to release proxy ports right before spawning proxies.
- Reduced cross-test interference by suffixing ha-e2e test_name/cluster_name/scope with a unique token in src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs.
- Added focused unit tests in src/test_harness/ports.rs for release_port semantics.
- Next: implement step 2 (split ultra-long no-quorum fencing stress into multiple short real-binary tests) + step 3 Makefile timeout enforcement, then run full gates and finish task.
